accepted_inputs: A production-agent failure with logs, receipts, trace links, or screenshots.
An audit gap where a team cannot prove why an agent action was allowed, blocked, or billed.
A claim type that should require a receipt before an agent can say it completed, published, verified, or changed state.
A recurring agent reliability failure that should become a deterministic contract or proof artifact.
minimum_evidence: One concrete artifact: log excerpt, receipt, trace id, screenshot, or public link
rejected_inputs: Generic AI news, product announcements, or broad research requests.
Legal advice, regulated financial advice, or requests requiring undisclosed credentials.
Anything that asks Shadow to publish private customer data without redaction.

receipt_gate.name: EvidenceGate

policies.description: code/system mutation claims require same-turn tool evidence or a verifiable artifact
published-artifact claims require a live fetch or captured verification output
commit/push completion requires a cited SHA that resolves in git
definitive state assertions require a ground-truth read this turn

receipt_gate.rule: No supported receipt means no operational claim.

response_contract.falsifier: If this surface produces no concrete submitted agent failure within 7 days of publication, the intake wedge failed and should be reworked or retired.
possible_outputs: paid audit handoff when the failure needs deeper work
